pattern: Count maximal runs of non-space characters by counting the transitions from a space (or the start) into a non-space character.
hints: A segment is just a block of non-space characters; you need to count how many such blocks there are.
A new segment begins exactly where a non-space character follows a space or the very start of the string.
Scan left to right and increment a counter each time the current character is non-space and the previous character was a space (or there is no previous character).

edge_inputs.description: several words separated by single spaces
a single word with no spaces
leading and trailing spaces around words must not inflate the count
multiple consecutive spaces between words count as one separator

stress.description: random mix of words and runs of spaces
stress.note: spaces interleaved randomly so segment boundaries vary
